Title: The R2R3 MYB transcription factor PavMYB10.1 involves in anthocyanin biosynthesis and determines fruit skin colour in sweet cherry (Prunus avium L.)

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a cofactor with PavMYB10.1a in promoting anthocyanin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavbHLH interacted with functional PavMYB10.1a but not truncated PavMYB10.1b. 2. It is part of the putative MBW complex linked to activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis genes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-regulatory component for anthocyanin engineering, though not independently established as causal in this study
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment responsible for red fruit skin colour
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified by reference to orthologous genes of MdbHLH3; included as an interacting cofactor rather than the primary mapped causal gene.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a cofactor with PavMYB10.1a in promoting anthocyanin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavWD40 interacted with functional PavMYB10.1a but not truncated PavMYB10.1b. 2. It is part of the putative MBW complex linked to activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis genes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-regulatory component for anthocyanin engineering, though not independently established as causal in this study
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment responsible for red fruit skin colour
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified by reference to orthologous genes of MdWD40; included as an interacting cofactor rather than the primary mapped causal gene.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family; R2 and R3 DNA-binding domains
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and determines fruit skin colour; intact allele PavMYB10.1a confers dark-red fruit, truncated PavMYB10.1b confers blush fruit, and absent allele PavMYB10.1c confers yellow fruit
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis; branch activation during fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via binding target promoters as part of an MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PavANS and PavUFGT expression in ripening fruit
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit ripening stage; light-responsive AE-box context; ABA and light proposed in discussion as likely upstream signals
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a putative MBW complex with PavbHLH and PavWD40; PavMYB10.1a interacts with PavbHLH and PavWD40, PavMYB10.1b does not
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic mapping in F1 population; allele sequencing; expression profiling across cultivars and development; correlation with anthocyanin content; yeast two-hybrid; chromatin immunoprecipitation; subcellular localization; inheritance analysis across crosses; marker-trait association across 29 varieties
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major determinant of fruit skin colour and key regulator of anthocyanin accumulation in sweet cherry fruit skin
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Fruit-specific, strongly induced at 6–8 weeks after full bloom in Lapins and Rainier; undetectable in leaves and flowers; absent in Big Dragon
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit-specific, strongly induced at 6–8 weeks after full bloom in ripening fruit; undetectable in leaves and flowers
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage/ripening (6–8 weeks after full bloom); light-responsive promoter elements; field-grown plants
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for GFP localization construct; native PavANS and PavUFGT promoters contain AE-box light-responsive elements
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: SLAF-seq for locus mapping; Raw PavMYB10.1 cDNA and genomic DNA sequences deposited in GenBank under KP455680, KP455681, KP455682 and KP455683
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reliable DNA marker for selecting yellow, blush, and dark-red fruit skin colour; useful target for marker-assisted breeding and biotechnological enhancement of anthocyanin content or fruit colour
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavMYB10.1 was mapped to the fruit-colour locus on LG3 and its allelic state cosegregated with dark-red, blush, and yellow skin phenotypes. 2. PavMYB10.1 expression rose sharply during ripening and was positively correlated with anthocyanin accumulation and with PavANS/PavUFGT transcript levels. 3. PavMYB10.1a interacted with PavbHLH and PavWD40 and was recruited to PavANS and PavUFGT promoters, supporting direct activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis genes. 4. PavMYB10.1 serves as a practical DNA marker for fruit skin colour breeding in sweet cherry.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment responsible for red fruit skin colour
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three functional alleles were defined: PavMYB10.1a intact functional allele, PavMYB10.1b truncated due to 1-bp deletion causing premature stop, and PavMYB10.1c undetectable/absent in yellow varieties. PavMYB10.1a is dominant to PavMYB10.1b and PavMYB10.1b is dominant to PavMYB10.1c.
